title: LMS/OER-repo ingest adapter for a confirmed consumer's format
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. An adapter pinned to a confirmed consumer's preferred format; built only once a named consumer commits, so verifiedNeed stays false until then.
objective: Build an LMS/OER-repo ingest adapter that emits crosswalk records in a confirmed consumer's preferred format.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter exports/ingests crosswalk records in a confirmed consumer's preferred format (pins to partner-016's recorded format).
Round-trips identifiers without loss; validated against the consumer's spec with fixtures.
verifiedNeed flips to true only when a named consumer commits; until then it is representative/blocked.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
